content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — both usage strings are inadequate: NSCameraUsageDescription is generic ("This app needs camera access." — Info.plist:8) and NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a placeholder ("TODO" — Info.plist:10); no location feature exists in the code.
Pierre: Apple rejects purpose strings that are empty, placeholder, or too vague to explain why the data is needed and which feature uses it. The camera string does not mention document scanning (the actual feature in ScannerView.swift), and the location string is literally "TODO" with no location-using code visible at all. Rewrite the camera string to reference document scanning, and either remove the location key or add a specific string tied to a real, visible location feature.
